seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in Flowise Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in Flowise agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Flowise, AI agent hallucinations manifest as fabricated facts or illogical responses in chatflows, often due to unvalidated intermediate outputs from LLM chains, irrelevant vector store retrievals, silent custom tool failures, or context loss in multi-agent handoffs. These issues are amplified in Flowise's drag-and-drop interface where developers may overlook validation nodes between components. Vex provides structured detection by intercepting Flowise's execution traces at key checkpoints.

howItManifests.scenario: Chatflow chains producing unvalidated intermediate outputs
howItManifests.symptom: LLM Chain node outputs JSON with missing fields or fabricated data structures
howItManifests.impact: Downstream Conditional Router nodes route to wrong branches, causing incorrect final responses

howItManifests.scenario: Vector store retrieval returning low-relevance documents
howItManifests.symptom: Retriever node returns documents with similarity scores < 0.7 but no filtering applied
howItManifests.impact: LLM generates answers based on irrelevant context, producing confident but wrong factual claims

howItManifests.scenario: Custom tool nodes failing silently without error propagation
howItManifests.symptom: Custom Function Tool returns empty string or null without throwing errors
howItManifests.impact: Agent continues execution with missing data, hallucinating tool results in final response

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-agent sequential flow losing context between agent handoffs
howItManifests.symptom: Agent handoff nodes drop memory context, causing subsequent agents to generate unrelated responses
howItManifests.impact: Final answer contradicts earlier conversation context, appearing as hallucination

detection.name: Intermediate Output Schema Validation
detection.description: Detects hallucinated JSON structures from LLM Chain nodes by validating against expected schemas

detection.name: Vector Relevance Score Threshold Check
detection.description: Monitors Conversational Retrieval QA Chain nodes for low-relevance document retrieval

detection.name: Custom Tool Empty Response Monitor
detection.description: Flags custom function tools returning empty/null results without errors

fixes.name: Add Output Parser Node
fixes.description: Insert structured output parser after LLM Chain nodes to enforce schema compliance

fixes.name: Retriever Filtering Node
fixes.description: Add Document Filter node after retriever with minimum similarity threshold

fixes.name: Tool Error Boundary Wrapper
fixes.description: Wrap custom tools with error boundary that returns structured failure responses

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as a callback handler in Flowise chatflows, automatically intercepting node outputs during execution. It runs the three detection methods above against every LLMChain, Retriever, and Tool node execution, flagging hallucinations in real-time with precise node-level attribution. 

When hallucinations are detected, Vex can automatically trigger remediation flows like re-running with corrected prompts, injecting validation nodes, or routing to human-in-the-loop review. The platform maintains execution lineage, enabling pattern analysis across deployments to prevent recurring hallucination types.

Vex's Flowise integration requires zero code changes—just add the callback in chatflow configuration. It captures full execution traces including intermediate outputs, metadata scores, and context windows for comprehensive hallucination root cause analysis.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex in 2 minutes to automatically detect and remediate Flowise hallucinations across all your chatflows.
